purpose: The header region of an Object Page that identifies and summarizes the object. Contains the title, subtitle, key attributes, statuses, and primary actions. Sits at the top of Object Page layouts.
whenToUse: As the header of an Object Page (via ObjectPageDynamicHeaderTitle)
In a Fullscreen Dialog that shows a single object
As the header of a Card that summarizes an object
Anywhere the user needs to see 'what am I looking at?' at a glance
whenNotToUse: For a list header — use DynamicPageTitle with count instead
For a small object preview — use ObjectListItem in a list
For a modal action confirmation — use Dialog title alone
Standalone as a page — Object Header is a region, not a page
doRules: Show 1-3 key attributes maximum in the visible header
Include an image or Avatar for identity when available
Pair status text with icon (no state-by-color-alone)
Include primary actions in the actions slot
dontRules: Do not exceed 5 status indicators — becomes noisy
Do not put long descriptions in the header — use the content area
Do not hide the object title — always visible in snapped state
Do not use in place of DynamicPageTitle for list views

layoutGuidance.placement: Top of Object Page, above sections
layoutGuidance.alignment: Title left-aligned; actions right-aligned; attributes in horizontal wrap

contentGuidance.labelLength: Title up to 100 chars; subtitle up to 200 chars; attributes ≤ 40 chars each
contentGuidance.contentRules: Title is a name or ID; subtitle is descriptive; attributes are key-value or numeric
examples: Purchase Order PO-48865 · Supplier ABC Corp · Status: Pending

responsiveBehavior.M: Attributes stack to a single column
responsiveBehavior.S: Snapped state by default; expand on tap

requirements: Title as H1 (or semantic heading role)
Actions have accessible names (aria-label if icon-only)
Status indicators have icon + text (not color-only)
